  • Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills, with a deep understanding of troubleshooting methodologies and best practices.
  • Experience working with REST APIs, scripting, and containers
  • Passion for working with technical customers and guiding teams to resolve complex issues.
  • Ability to analyze support trends and implement strategies to enhance customer and engineer experience.

  • Familiarity with security automation, IT workflows, or related domains.

  • Experience providing technical support to US Federal or Public Sector organizations

This role is an excellent opportunity to develop technical leadership skills to drive the success of a growing support team, influence product development, and contribute to the long-term success of our customers.

Target Annual Compensation: $130-145K

Applicants for this opportunity must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

About the Role

Enterprise Access is the authentication and authorization backbone of the Okta Identity Cloud. Every login, every policy evaluation, every federation flow runs through this pillar. The products MFA, SSO, Authentication Policies, Authenticators, Federations (SAML/OIDC/OAuth), XAA Protocol, Customer Identity, Identity Verification, Frontline Worker Authentication, and OIE Upgrade paths,  serve billions of monthly authentications across thousands of customers. This PM Director will own the authentication protocols and policies that enable human and non-human identities to authenticate, delegate authority, and interact securely with enterprise resources.

You will run Enterprise Access day-to-day: setting roadmap, driving execution, managing a team of 5-7 PMs, and serving as the primary PM voice for this pillar in quarterly planning, cross-pillar coordination, and customer engagements. Success in this role requires a strong ability to understand customer requirements, a deep empathy for user experience, and the ability to strategically prioritize what’s most important while managing requests from multiple teams at Okta.

  • Partner with the data team to define product usage metrics and tie them to business outcomes.
  • Prioritize rigorously. This portfolio has more demand than capacity. Saying no clearly is as important as saying yes.

Customer Engagement

jobs.title: Director, Okta Threat Intelligence
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. Okta is the World’s Identity Company. 

Okta’s workforce and customer identity platforms are trusted to secure access to resources for workforce users, partners and end customers.

As a trusted supplier to some of the world’s most critical organizations, Okta is uniquely positioned to understand the threat environment. 

Okta Threat Intelligence is the capability within Okta that delivers timely insights to customers that are both highly relevant and actionable, and that contribute to public conversations about identity-based attacks. Our research reinforces customer confidence in Okta’s best-in-class security capabilities.

We are looking for a highly motivated individual contributor to work at the intersection of identity-based attacks and agentic AI.

The successful candidate requires strong analytical, communication and interpersonal skills, as well as cyber domain knowledge. 

Core Responsibilities

  • Gather intelligence requirements from multiple stakeholders
  • Collaborate with product engineering and security teams to anonymize, aggregate and derive threat-related insights from platform data
  • Identify and research emerging or novel identity-based threats in order to produce actionable intelligence for customers and the broader community
  • Coordinate the distribution of finished intelligence, seeking new and creative ways of making our insights more accessible and actionable for customers and partners
  • Brief Okta’s strategic customers about the threat environment, and handle inbound inquiries where required
  • Measure the impact of intelligence and analysis products on customer adoption and secure configuration

What does it take?

You lead by example and influence. The most creative and technically gifted people in the room want to work with you and for you. 

You’re a team player. You prefer to work in a team where every colleague rolls up their sleeves and contributes equally. 

You have great communications skills and boundless curiosity. 

You write succinctly and speak persuasively. 

You work with purpose. You are motivated by the prospect of driving security best practices in a company that protects millions of people. 

You have a demonstrated ability to evaluate risk and to produce finished intelligence under tight deadlines.

Requirements

  • A degree in a relevant field would be beneficial but is not strictly required. We value real-world experience and demonstrable skills and influence in the cyber domain
  • Evidence of strong written communication and presentation skills 
  • Evidence of strong data analysis and scripting skills (predominantly using SQL and Python)
  • Demonstrable use of AI workflows or agents to accelerate your research and analysis
  • Ability to handle sensitive information with a high degree of integrity and discretion

jobs.title: Digital Growth & Personalization Manager

jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Role Overview

As a Digital Growth & Personalization Manager, you'll be the strategic advisor and thought partner to our Digital Strategy team—helping define what to test, why it matters, and what the results mean for our customers. You'll own the intersection of customer research, testing strategy, and stakeholder guidance, building a testing roadmap grounded in deep understanding of B2B buyer journeys. This is a hands-on strategic role where you'll launch tests yourself, present findings, and become the trusted expert voice of site optimization and personalization. 

Responsibilities

  • Test and personalization strategy: Define what should be tested and personalized and why. Guide teams with optimization best practices and refine test hypotheses with stakeholders to ensure rigor.
  • Own roadmap management: Build and manage a quarterly testing roadmap grounded in customer research and business priorities. 
  • Customer journey research: Initiate your own research to understand friction points, user segments, and behavior patterns across our B2B buyer journey. Synthesize findings into actionable testing hypotheses and personas.
  • Hands-on test ownership: Launch and manage tests independently (copy changes, simple targeting, basic segments) while collaborating with engineering on more complex builds. Own test plans, success criteria, and validation.
  • Insights & analysis: Dig beyond winner/loser narratives. Uncover segment-specific lift, identify which experiences resonate with which buyer profiles, and translate findings into the next phase of strategy.
  • Stakeholder leadership & storytelling: Present test ideas, progress, and conclusions. Frame personalization and testing wins as drivers of sales cycle efficiency and user experience. Become the trusted advisor on digital strategy and buyer behavior.
  • Technical fluency & collaboration: Understand data layers, tracking requirements, and technical frameworks well enough to brief engineers and unlock deeper personalization triggers. You won't be implementing—but you'll speak the language.

Necessary Experience

  • 5+ years of growth, experimentation, or optimization experience with a track record of managing testing roadmaps and building strategic plans across marketing functions.
  • Segmentation & targeting expertise: You understand how to define audiences, test hypotheses against specific segments, and identify which experiences move the needle for which buyer profiles.
  • Personalization campaign management: You’ve created new campaigns for web properties, reported progress, and iteratively improved personalization strategies.
  • Customer research & synthesis: You’re data-first, and seek to understand customer journeys and translate findings into testing strategy.
  • Testing platform expertise: Hands-on experience launching and managing tests through tools like Adobe Target is a plus. (Optimizely, VWO, experience with other testing platforms is required).
  • Strategic stakeholder communication: You've presented test ideas, results, and strategic recommendations to Directors and Sr. Directors. You know how to frame testing wins as business outcomes, not just metrics.
  • Technical literacy: You understand data layers, tracking, and how personalization gets built and can launch simple tests (copy, basic targeting) independently.
  • B2B mindset: Experience in B2B environments, understanding longer sales cycles, multiple decision-makers, and the buyer journey complexity that shapes testing strategy.

Preferred Experience

  • B2B martech stack: Familiarity with tools like Marketo, Demandbase, 6sense, or similar—used to inform targeting and audience strategy.
  • Cross-organization collaborator: You've successfully shaped testing strategy or roadmaps as a peer advisor, pushing back on ideas

jobs.title: Digital Content & AI Automation Manager
jobs.location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; Toronto, Ontario, Canada
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Role Summary

We’re looking for a Digital Content & AI Automation Manager to lead our transition to 'Vibe Marketing.' Inspired by the shift toward Vibe Coding, we want to bridge the gap between creative intent and digital execution. You’ll move Okta away from manual tickets and rigid CMS forms toward an agentic, self-service model—building the tools that allow marketers to describe a goal in natural language and see it manifest across our digital ecosystem.

This is a test, learn, build, iterate role. You aren't just managing a stack; you’re building an autonomous engine that empowers every marketer to be a creator.

What You’ll Actually Do

  • Build the "Vibe Marketing" tools: Create the workflows and agentic layers that allow non-technical stakeholders to manage and scale digital content across its entire lifecycle using natural language and AI assistance.
  • Develop Agentic Support Models: Prototype and deploy AI agents that act as "co-pilots" for Marketing Managers—handling everything from automated tagging and metadata to complex distribution across our headless ecosystem.
  • Architect Self-Service Systems: Reduce the "time-to-live" by building modular, reusable content structures that agents can manipulate, moving us beyond the constraints of traditional platforms like Adobe Experience Manager.
  • Operate Like a Lab: Run rapid experiments with emerging AI tools and automation platforms. Build scrappy prototypes—not just strategy decks—to prove that "vibe-based" creation works at enterprise scale.
  • Partner Across Teams: Bridge the gap between "what if" and "here’s how," working with Marketing, Engineering, and Digital teams to turn agentic concepts into production-ready workflows.

What We’re Looking For

  • The "Vibe" Mindset: You understand the shift from "doing the work" to "directing the agents." You’ve followed the Vibe Coding movement and are itching to apply it to Marketing Tech.
  • 4–8 Years of Experience: You’ve seen the limitations of legacy CMS architectures and are ready to bypass those bottlenecks by building a modern, agent-led content supply chain.
  • Hands-on AI & Automation: You don't just prompt; you build. You have experience with APIs, MCP, LLM orchestration, and connecting tools to create seamless loops.
  • Technical Fluency: You aren't a full-stack engineer, but you understand how to navigate enterprise complexity—you're comfortable with structured content, and the logic of how data flows between a headless CMS, an LLM, and the front-end.

Okta's Enterprise Data & Insights team powers the data infrastructure that drives decision-making across the company by building reliable pipelines, scalable data platforms, and production-grade data products. We partner closely with internal Data and Insights Analysts as well as external Okta Product teams to unlock business value through robust data architecture, efficient data movement, and the engineering foundations that make data trustworthy at scale.       

 

The Senior DevOps Data Engineer Opportunity

Okta's Enterprise Data Platform and Engineering team builds the platforms and capabilities that power decision-making across the entire organization. As a DevOps Engineer on this team, you will be integral to building and sustaining strong relationships with our Engineering, Technical Operations, and Technology partners. You will be part of a team doing detailed technical designs and implementing applications using cutting-edge technology within a high-security, high-compliance environment.

  • Programming Skills: 3+ years of hands-on experience with Python, Go, or similar backend languages.
  • Security Mindset: Experience with system hardening, applying security standards like STIGs, and hands-on experience with vulnerability management tools and processes (e.g., Nessus, Qualys, Trivy). Relevant technical or security certifications are a plus.
  • Proven Automation Record: A demonstrated history of designing and building automated systems that streamline the software delivery lifecycle.
  • U.S. Person Status: This position requires the ability to access federal environments. As a condition of employment, you must be able to submit documentation establishing U.S. Person status (e.g., U.S. Citizen, Lawful Permanent Resident, Refugee, or Asylee) upon hire.

The Buying Programs Strategy & Operations Team

 

Okta’s Buying Programs Strategy & Operations team is responsible for closing complex commercial contracts by providing Sales teams with business process and policy insights, as well as strategic and operational support. 

The team sits within the broader Go-To-Market Strategy and Operations organization, which drives strategy through execution for Okta’s durable growth. As a member of GTM S&O, you will have the opportunity to learn about and contribute to Okta's business from all angles, including standardizing policies and systems to scale operations as well as collaborating with product teams to roll out new offerings.

The Manager, Deal Strategy Opportunity

In this role, your primary function will be to strategically and effectively model and structure non-standard proposals and contracts that mutually benefit Okta and its Customers in EMEA. You will be well-positioned as the “pulse” of the business and strategic adviser to the Sales organization. If you have a successful track record of thriving in ambiguity within a rapidly growing environment and solving complex problems, this is your role.

Serve as an expert on deal structuring, pricing, commercial terms, processes, and policiesEnsure all non-standard commercial terms are reviewed, approved, and documented correctly in accordance with Revenue Operations requirementsStrategize with Sales and internal stakeholders, including Accounting, Revenue Operations, Legal, Compliance, and Product to structure and position complex dealsProvide Revenue Operations and Accounting teams with context and clarification on complex deal structures and metric treatment related to booking, invoicing, internal reporting, etc.

  • Minimum 5 years of Sales support experience in the technology industry with at least 3 years experience in a Deal Desk role
  • A willingness to enforce deal governance and compliance policies with a thorough understanding of the SaaS revenue model and reporting 
  • Demonstrated project management experience or participation in process improvement efforts
  • Excellent communication skills, written & verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly and effectively across all levels of the organization
  • Proven performance in negotiating and closing large enterprise SaaS agreements 
  • Aptitude in prioritizing, maintaining attention to detail, and producing timely and quality work under high pressure and tight deadlines with the capacity to lead and engage in multiple initiatives

jobs.title: Data Quality Analyst II
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Detail-oriented and results-driven Data Quality Analyst II with an extensive background in improving data accuracy, completeness, and consistency in our CRM (Salesforce). Apt at identifying data quality issues, developing processes to clean and maintain data, and providing actionable insights to enhance the overall customer data management strategy. Proficient in data governance, data profiling, and leveraging tools to ensure optimal data integrity for business operations

Responsibilities

  • Data Enrichment: Regularly audit and clean account data, hierarchies, and territory alignments within Salesforce to ensure accuracy, completeness, and relevance.
  • Data Quality Monitoring: Manage deduplication processes, data validation, and ongoing quality reporting.
  • Data Governance: Ensure data management practices align with internal policies, compliance requirements, and industry best practices.
  • Process Improvement: Identify, document, and raise data quality issues regarding system processes and procedures.
  • Collaboration: Work cross-functionally with global sales, marketing, and regional teams to drive data issues to resolution.
  • Reporting: Create and modify Salesforce reports to suit localized and global field needs.
  • Ticketing Systems & Support: Provide timely guidance to end-users with data entry, account creation, and general system requests.

Experience

  • Minimum of 3+ years of professional hands-on experience in a Data Quality or similar data management role. 
  • Ensuring strict SLAs are met and all data support requests are driven to full resolution.
  • Successfully leading data quality improvement initiatives for Salesforce to optimize data accuracy
  • Conducting regular data audits to identify duplicates, missing information, and other discrepancies, while swiftly implementing corrective actions.
  • Creating automated reports in Salesforce to flag potential data issues in real-time.

You are used to:

  • Managing and structuring large datasets to prepare them for analytics and mass system uploads.
  • Working with cross-functional to transform data and increase overall system integrity.
  • Developing dynamic dashboards and reports to track actionable key data quality metrics.
  • Thriving in a flexible work environment, managing a hybrid schedule (2 days in-office, 3 days WFH), and successfully handling PST/rotational shift dynamics.
  • Consistently adhering to company policies and maintaining a high standard of professional communication (both written and verbal).
  • Multitasking efficiently between urgent, ad-hoc requests and daily operational commitments.

You are known for:

  • Attention to Detail: Exceptional focus on data accuracy and an innate ability to spot minor discrepancies or inconsistencies.
  • Problem-Solving Skills: Creative and proactive in developing sustainable solutions to data-related issues and improving legacy data processes.
  • Process Documentation: Ability to clearly document workflows, guidelines, and best practices for maintaining long-term data integrity.

Technical Skills

  • CRM System: Salesforce (Required)
  • Data Manipulation: Advanced Excel / Google Sheets
  • Data Querying: Salesforce Reporting & Dashboards
  • Data Quality Tools: DemandTools, DataLoader, SalesforceInspector
  • Data Enrichment Tools: Dun & Bradstreet (D&B), ZoomInfo, Traction Hierarchies

Preferred Skills (Optional)

  • Data Visualization: Anaplan
  • Advanced Data Querying: SQL / SOQL

Certifications (Optional)

  • Salesforce Certified Administrator or Salesforce Certified Data Architect.

Qualifications:

Degree: Bachelor’s at minimum with 3+ yrs

jobs.title: Data Governance & Workday Reporting Specialist
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Role Overview

As a senior-level individual contributor, you will establish and scale enterprise-grade data governance while leading advanced Workday analytics. You will bridge the gap between People Operations, Finance, and IT to ensure data integrity, regulatory compliance (SOX, GDPR), and innovation through Gen AI-driven reporting.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data Governance & Stewardship

  • Framework Ownership: Define and maintain enterprise data standards, policies, and ownership models.

  • Compliance & Privacy: Partner with Legal and Security to ensure alignment with GDPR and HIPAA.

  • Documentation: Manage data dictionaries, metadata, and lineage to ensure transparent data accountability.

2. Advanced Workday Reporting & Analytics

  • Report Architecture: Design complex Advanced, Matrix, Composite, and Trending reports using Workday Prism and Discovery Boards.

  • System Optimization: Build scalable calculated fields and optimize report performance across HCM, Payroll, and Finance.

  • Insights: Translate complex business requirements into actionable, high-performance dashboards for leadership.

3. Compliance, SOX & Audit

  • Control Management: Maintain SOX controls, ITGCs, and automated data checks to ensure "audit-ready" reporting.

  • Risk Mitigation: Support internal audits, monitor control deficiencies, and lead timely remediation of gaps.

  • Access Reviews: Oversee user access and Segregation of Duties (SoD) reporting.

4. Data Quality & Integrity

  • Automated Oversight: Develop automated exception reporting and quality checks to proactively detect anomalies.

  • Root Cause Analysis: Lead investigations into data inconsistencies and implement permanent remediation strategies.

  • Continuous Improvement: Drive the evolution of data governance maturity across all HR systems.

5. Strategic Collaboration

  • Business Partnership: Consult with People and Finance leaders to provide data-driven recommendations.

  • Literacy & Training: Upskill business users on reporting tools and data best practices to foster a data-centric culture.

Qualifications

Required:

  • Experience: 8-10+ years in Data Governance, HRIS, or Analytics; 5-8+ years of hands-on

jobs.title: Data Engineering Manager
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

The Data & Insights Team

Our focus is on building platforms, capabilities, and insights that are utilized across the organization by sales, marketing, engineering, finance, product, and operations. The ideal candidate will have a strong data and development background with the ability to tie data engineering and analytics initiatives to business impact. 

You will be part of a team that empowers the enterprise with trusted, timely, and actionable insights to drive business transformation and customer value.

What you’ll be doing 

Team Leadership & Development

  • Lead, mentor, and coach a team of data engineers, cultivating a culture of ownership, accountability, and continuous improvement.
  • Conduct regular one-on-one meetings, performance reviews, and career development discussions with team members.
  • Drive recruiting and hiring efforts to build and maintain a high-performing engineering team.
  • Report on team progress, challenges, and technical roadmaps to the U.S. based manager.

Technical Execution & Guidance

  • Provide clear technical guidance and direction, ensuring the team adheres to best practices in data engineering, testing, and deployment.
  • Act as a hands-on data engineering contributor, directly involved in the design, development, and maintenance of scalable data pipelines and infrastructure.
  • Participate in architectural reviews and technical design discussions, making sound and timely technical decisions.
  • Ensure the team maintains rigorous standards for code quality, system performance, and reliability across all systems.
  • Optimize the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) and deployment processes to enhance efficiency and speed of delivery.

Project & Operational Management

  • Manage project timelines, resource allocation, and deliverables utilizing project management tracking software (e.g., Jira).
  • Anticipate, identify, and proactively mitigate risks and issues that could impact project timelines or team performance.
  • Drive seamless cross-functional collaboration with other engineering teams, QA, and operations to ensure smooth product integration and release.
  • Collaborate closely with Product Management and other key stakeholders to define the team's technical roadmap and strategic vision.

Support Management

  • Manage support request triage for L1-L3 support and ensure timely prioritization and resolution.
  • Own global support process and operations - weekly on-call rotation, responding and troubleshooting, and resolving critical incidents and production issues -  partnering with engineering and analytics management for implementation and process improvements.
  • Ensure the global support process is adhered to by both direct staff and across the organization.

What you’ll bring to the role

Required Qualifications & Experience:

  • Education: BS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
  • Engineering & Leadership: 8+ years of experience in data engineering and/or software development, including 2+ years in a formal people management role.
  • Team Management: Proven ability to lead and mentor a team of 4 or more engineers.

Support Management: Experience managing a global support process across multiple teams and timezones

Technical Expertise:

  • Data Lifecycle Mastery: Demonstrated understanding of the full lifecycle for data assets, encompassing the building, development, and maintenance of data models, reporting systems, data automation, dashboards, and performance metrics crucial for driving key business decisions.
  • Problem Solving: Ability to design and implement robust technical processes to effectively solve business problems.
  • Database Skills: Proficiency in Relational Database design, implementation, querying, and reporting (DDL, SQL).
  • Data Tools: 3+ years of pract

jobs.title: Customer Audit Manager
jobs.location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; San Francisco, California; Washington, DC
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

The Customer Trust Team

The Okta Security team’s mission is to strengthen Okta’s position as the leading Identity-as-a-Service solution by identifying and resolving risks to the employees, product, and most importantly, our customers.The Customer Trust team works to enhance customer trust in the identity products our teams build. We serve as a strategic resource working closely with Okta’s GTM teams.

We are also the voice of Okta Security, both externally and internally. It's our role to influence how the Okta security brand is perceived and to shape the security culture of the company. Through our continued focus and partnership, we aim to ensure that:

  1. When people think of Okta, they think of a leading zero trust cybersecurity company
  2. When people work at Okta, they role model the best security behaviors to meet the high standard we set for security inside the company.

The Customer Audit Manager Opportunity

Demonstrating Okta’s security program to customers and prospects is a core responsibility of the Customer Trust team, and to meet rapidly growing demand, we are seeking a Manager of Customer Audit to lead this capability of our team. This role will be responsible for creating a customer & prospect audit program, maintaining an evidence collection for presentation, coordinating and hosting security audits both virtually and on-site in our San Francisco Executive Briefing Center, and other locations in North America and globally as required.

Okta’s Customer Trust team is a highly technical group, and the ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with the technical implementation of SOC2 and ISO controls in a cloud-based environment, while maintaining the ability to describe the effectiveness and risk mitigation of these controls to an auditor. The candidate will also have the ability to train and manage employees globally to support Okta’s international growth. This unique position has the opportunity to not only interact with the security teams of Fortune 500 companies, but also create a highly visible example for exceptional third-party risk assessments.

What you’ll be doing 

  • Create and communicate a strategy to deliver Okta Security’s audit program, maintaining a stakeholder group across the business and security team
  • Cooperate with the Security Compliance team to create and maintain an evidence repository for use in third-party risk audits
  • Coordinate and schedule customer or prospect onsite and virtual audits
  • Understand customer or prospect risk concerns and lead the presentation of evidence material to resolve these concerns
  • Interact with the Customer Trust team to develop and publish content regarding Okta’s security controls 
  • Coordinate between Customer Trust, the GRC team and other Okta stakeholders to document, manage, track, and proactively communicate ownership and status updates regarding audit observations accepted by Okta, between those groups and also back to the customer or prospect
  • Collaborate with Okta Security on other projects, as applicable

What you’ll bring to the role

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or Management Information Systems, or equivalent work experience
  • In-depth knowledge of IT security frameworks and best practices, such as NIST SP800 publications, CoBIT, CCM, and Trust Principles and Criteria
  • Experience in IT regulation and compliance standards such as SOC 1 and 2, ISO27001, and ISO42001
  • Working knowledge of terms and concepts used in information security, privacy,  risk assessments, and contingency planning
  • Understanding of IT and cloud methodologies, such as software development lifecycle and operations
  • Excellent oral, written, and presentation skill

Helpful Certifications / Skills:

  • Certified Information Security Auditor (CISA)
  • GIAC Security Essentials (GSEC)
  • Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP)
  • Certificate of Cloud Security Knowledge (CCSK)
  • Certificate of Cloud Auditing Knowledge (CCAK)

jobs.title: Corporate Development Manager, M&A
jobs.location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; San Francisco, California; Seattle, Washington
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

As the world accelerates towards an AI-driven, hyper-connected landscape, Okta's mission to enable secure identity for any technology has never been more critical. Our Corporate Development team is the strategic engine that will power Okta's inorganic growth, ensuring we stay ahead of the curve. We are looking for a Corporate Development Manager to be a key player in this mission, identifying and executing acquisitions that will define the future of identity.

In this role, you will move beyond traditional M&A execution. You will be a market expert, developing investment theses on emerging landscapes like AI-driven security, decentralized identity, and machine-to-machine trust. You will work with Okta's senior leadership to not only buy companies but to buy capabilities that expand our platform, accelerate our roadmap, and solidify our position as the world's identity standard.

What you’ll achieve:

  • Develop data-driven market research and investment theses on new growth areas, presenting your recommendations to product and executive leadership to influence Okta’s long-term strategy.
  • Source high-potential startups for acquisition, investment, or partnership, and build a strong presence across the founder and VC ecosystem.
  • Drive the entire M&A lifecycle for strategic acquisitions, from sourcing and initial evaluation to valuation, due diligence, and deal execution, in partnership with senior team members.
  • Drive work streams across diligence, valuation, financial modeling, strategic assessment, and internal recommendations
  • Partner with Product and Engineering leaders to evaluate build/buy/partner opportunities aligned with Okta and Auth0 roadmaps
  • Collaborate on integration planning with cross-functional partners after acquisitions or strategic deals
  • Use new technologies/AI to hunt for signals in the noise: from founders, funds, internal teams, and the broader market; and translate it into action.

What you’ll bring to the role:

  • 5+ years of experience in corporate development, investment banking (TMT focus), strategy consulting, or other relevant roles with a strong track record of deal execution.
  • A strong foundation in financial analysis, valuation, and modeling, with a deep, intuitive understanding of SaaS business models and metrics.
  • Proven experience working on complex M&A or strategic investment projects, ideally within the enterprise software, cloud, or security sectors.
  • Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with a natural ability to distill complex topics and build consensus among executives and cross-functional teams.
  • Intellectual curiosity and a passion for technology, with the ability to think strategically about product-market fit, technical challenges, and competitive moats.
  • Comfort navigating ambiguity with a bias toward building structured execution and always seeking opportunities for continuous improvement.
  • Familiarity with generative AI models (ChatGPT, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.) and prompt development
  • Experience/familiarity with Slack, Apple MacOS and GSuite.

In addition, Okta offers equity (where applicable), bonus, and benefits, including health, dental and vision insurance, 401(k), flexible spending account, and paid leave (including PTO and parental leave) in accordance with our applicable plans and policies. jobs.title: Corporate Counsel, IP
jobs.location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Okta is the World’s Identity Company. As a cloud-first SaaS provider, our mission is to enable any organization to use any technology by providing world-class security and identity solutions. The Okta Legal team is a high-impact group of professionals dedicated to navigating the complex landscape of technology law while fueling innovation. We are looking for a sharp, collaborative IP lawyer with a software engineering, computer science, or electrical engineering background to help protect our innovations and brand, and continuously shape and refine our global IP strategy.

More specifically, we seek to hire a Patent Attorney with a software and patent prosecution background and relevant technical degree (CS, EE, or ECE) to work with Okta’s legal and business teams to further develop Okta’s global patent portfolio and corresponding strategy. The scope of the role includes identifying patentable inventions and securing corresponding patent assets, providing expert technical and legal analysis, handling patent challenges, providing guidance on patent transactions and licensing matters, and supporting M&A and litigation. 

The ideal candidate will also have experience with or a strong interest in handling and/or supporting all other aspects of Okta’s intellectual property work, including trademark, copyright, and trade secrets and will support other legal projects as required.

Key Duties & Responsibilities

Portfolio Management & Strategic Advice

  • Oversee the lifecycle of Okta’s IP assets, ensuring alignment with corporate development and business goals.
  • Refine and execute offensive and defensive IP strategies to maintain Okta’s competitive edge in the identity market.
  • Serve as an advisor on IP-related business risks and emerging legal trends.
  • Translate complex IP concepts into business-oriented advice for non-legal stakeholders.
  • Refine company-wide IP policies, including invention disclosure programs and patent incentive schemes.

Patent 

  • Partner with engineering and product teams to identify patentable inventions and develop patent literacy among them.
  • Collaborate with outside counsel on the drafting and prosecution of high-quality patent applications.
  • Develop knowledge of industry’s patent landscape to guide patent strategy and approach to patent drafting.
  • Refine Okta’s patent strategy to ensure broad protection of our core technologies and to put forward-looking systems in place to identify and protect our next-generation ideas.

Other IP Issues

  • Advise on strategic trade secret issues and help implement related policies.
  • Help provide guidance on software copyright protection and the use of OSS within Okta products, and manage copyright claims.
  • Support the protection of the Okta and Auth0 brands through trademark registration and enforcement. 
  • Advise on interesting legal issues related to API integrations, digital content, professional services and developer agreements, and more.
  • Draft, review, and negotiate IP-specific provisions in MSAs, JDAs, vendor contracts, and licensing agreements.
  • Conduct IP due diligence for potential acquisitions and draft IP-related closing documents and transition service agreements.

Compliance and Policy

  • Assist with IP-related compliance issues and industry standards relevant to SaaS and cybersecurity.
  • Deliver training to employees on IP best practices and confidentiality.

Litigation 

  • Support all phases of IP litigation.

Qualifications

  • Education & Credentials:
    • J.D. from an accredited law school and active Bar membership.
    • Undergraduate or graduate degree(s) in computer science, electrical engineering, computer engineering, or the like.
    • USPTO Registered Patent Attorney.
  • Experience:
    • 5+ years of IP experience, including software patent prosecution.
  • Technical Savvy:
    • Experience with SaaS, cloud-based

  • Partner with engineering and product teams to identify patentable inventions and develop patent literacy among them.
  • Collaborate with outside counsel on the drafting and prosecution of high-quality patent applications.
  • Develop knowledge of industry’s patent landscape to guide patent strategy and approach to patent drafting.
  • Refine Okta’s patent strategy to ensure broad protection of our core technologies and to put forward-looking systems in place to identify and protect our next-generation ideas.

Other IP Issues

  • Advise on strategic trade secret issues and help implement related policies.
  • Help provide guidance on software copyright protection and the use of OSS within Okta products, and manage copyright claims.
  • Support the protection of the Okta and Auth0 brands through trademark registration and enforcement. 
  • Advise on interesting legal issues related to API integrations, digital content, professional services and developer agreements, and more.
  • Draft, review, and negotiate IP-specific provisions in MSAs, JDAs, vendor contracts, and licensing agreements.
  • Conduct IP due diligence for potential acquisitions and draft IP-related closing documents and transition service agreements.

Compliance and Policy

  • Assist with IP-related compliance issues and industry standards relevant to SaaS and cybersecurity.
  • Deliver training to employees on IP best practices and confidentiality.

Litigation 

  • Support all phases of IP litigation.

Qualifications

  • Education & Credentials:
    • J.D. from an accredited law school and active Bar membership.
    • Undergraduate or graduate degree(s) in computer science, electrical engineering, computer engineering, or the like.
    • USPTO Registered Patent Attorney.
  • Experience:
    • 5+ years of IP experience, including software patent prosecution.
  • Technical Savvy:
    • Experience with SaaS, cloud-based

jobs.title: Corporate Account Executive South Africa
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

The Okta Sales Team 

Okta has a vision to free anyone to safely use any technology by providing a secure, highly available, enterprise-grade platform that secures billions of Workforce and Customer log-ins every year. As an Okta AE, you will drive territory growth through both net new logos and cultivating relationships to develop and grow existing Okta Platform customers. With the support of your Okta ecosystem, your focus will be on consistent results and an unwavering commitment to our customers.

 

The South Africa Corporate Sales team is growing!

Our South Africa team is based out of Dublin and has seen huge transformation and growth in the last couple of years. We have an incredible opportunity to win the identity market with our already market leading technology.

 

The Corporate Sales Account Executive Opportunity - South Africa

 

The successful Okta Account Executive is a highly motivated, self-driven, and experienced Account Executive who is passionate about security and about driving protection against the biggest identity threats.

 

As an Okta AE, you will be focused on providing value to C-Suite decision makers on their ability to drive the most secure environment possible for their workforce. You will consistently drive territory growth through driving both net new logos and cultivating and growing existing Okta customers. You will continually drive territory growth through both net new logos as well as through cultivating relationships to develop and grow existing Okta & Auth0 customers.

 

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Establish a vision and plan to guide your long-term approach to net new logo pipeline generation
  • Consistently deliver revenue targets to support YoY territory growth
  • Identify, develop and execute account strategies to generate pipeline, drive sales opportunities and deliver repeatable and predictable bookings
  • Identify, target and gain access to appropriate leaders in prospect accounts, building and cultivating your network of decision makers 
  • Scope, negotiate and close agreements to consistently meet and exceed revenue quota targets
  • Holistically embrace, access, and utilize Okta partners to identify and open new, uncharted opportunities
  • Build and nurture effective working partnerships within your Okta ecosystem (xDRs, Partners, Presales, Customer First, etc)
  • Adopt a strong value based sales approach, always looking to bring a compelling point of view to each customer
  • Travel as necessary to build and cultivate customer and prospect relationships. Expectation to also be present in our Dublin office for collaboration and training purposes.

What you’ll bring to the role:

  • 3 + years success in growing revenue for sophisticated, complex enterprise SaaS solutions.
  • Ability to evangelize, educate and create demand with C-level decision makers
  • Ability to navigate complex sales cycles with multiple stakeholders from both the customer base and within the internal ecosystem
  • Proven success selling into C-suite and building partnership and buy-in with multiple stakeholders
  • Significant experience selling in partnership with GSI’s & the wider partner ecosystem
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills with audiences of all levels and all technical aptitudes
  • Confident and self driven with the humility required to successfully work in teams
  • Expertise using a Sales Framework such as MEDDICC, Challenger or Sandler (we use MEDDPICC)
  • Local language skills is a plus



As a Corporate Account Executive you will:

  • Position Okta Workforce cloud solutions to South African organisations.
  • Build and execute on a strong territory plan for your accounts in region
  • Target companies in the Corporate to lower Enterprise segment.
  • Focus on winning new logos with a land and expand mentality
  • Deliver outstanding customer experience to ensure renewals, and capitalise on upsell opportunities

jobs.title: CEO Communications Manager
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Title: CEO Communications Manager

San Francisco (in-office required) 

The CEO Communications Manager opportunity

We’re seeking a high-performing CEO Communications Manager to help amplify the voice and vision of our CEO and drive AI optimization across his communications channels. In this role, you will report to Okta’s Head of CEO Communications and partner closely with the Office of the CEO to support, streamline, and scale the CEO comms platform, spanning internal and external speaking events, media & influencer engagements, and social media. You will also work with the Corporate Communications team and partner with the broader executive bench, cross-functional partners, and our various agencies to develop bylines, blog posts, keynotes, talking points, and thought leadership content.

This is a rare opportunity to sit at the intersection of content, operations, strategy, and execution—all in support of a visionary founder leading a global tech company through transformation and growth. The ideal candidate is intellectually curious, highly accountable, and capable of grasping both business strategy as well as the underlying technology, particularly in the context of identity security, AI, and enterprise software. 

What you’ll be doing 

  • Content development: Develop and refine bylines, scripts, talking points, social copy, and briefing materials for internal and external engagements that faithfully reflect the CEO’s vision and voice across audiences ranging from employees and customers to global media and boards of directors.
  • Data driven optimization: Establish and maintain rigorous feedback loops and use engagement, sentiment, and reach data to continuously refine the CEO’s communication strategy, with an eye toward emerging trends in identity, AI, and enterprise tech.
  • Agentic innovation: Continuously apply and explore how AI and agents can securely optimize and automate workflows and content development across the CEO’s communications platform.
  • Event planning & production support: Serve as point of contact for production logistics across company events, including tech run-throughs, AV coordination, studio operations, and real-time event support.
  • Stakeholder management: Design and own streamlined, scalable workflows for the end-to-end content lifecycle—from ideation and drafting through cross-functional, legal, and PR review, to distribution. Manage inputs, timelines, and deliverables across multiple stakeholders, often coordinating between executives and production/design teams to ensure alignment across all CEO materials.
  • Operational excellence: The ideal candidate will thrive in and derive energy from a high-pressure, high-performing environment. Obsessive quality control and meticulous attention to detail are table stakes. You will anticipate needs, respond quickly, and act as a strategic thought partner—particularly in high-stakes, fast-paced settings. A bias for optimization and innovation will be highly valued.

What you’ll bring to the role & what we’re looking for

  • 5-7+ years of experience in corporate communications including direct experience with executive and/or CEO communications, ideally in a high-growth tech or SaaS company
  • Experience writing with and for CEOs, executives, leaders, and/or founders, with the ability to adapt to and maintain a distinct executive tone of voice
  • Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with high attention to detail
  • Experience with and eagerness to apply AI to enhance and reimagine processes while improving content quality and speed
  • A passion for operational excellence and ability to thrive in a high-performance environment, taking initiative, owning outcomes, and operating with polish and discretion
  • Strong project management instincts—capable of juggling shifting priorities, tight timelines, and multiple stakeholders with calm and precision
  • Desire to be part of a mission-driven team that values trust, humility, rigor, and creative ambition
  • An understanding of Ok

jobs.title: Category Manager, Indirect Spend
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Okta’s Strategic Sourcing and Procurement (SS&P) team is looking for an experienced Category Manager to join our team to co-lead global sourcing activities for indirect services. This includes leading projects and contract negotiations for various spend categories such as Professional Services (Consulting, Finance & Legal), Facilities, and HR. 

In this role, you will be a trusted partner to our stakeholders, guiding them throughout the entire sourcing process. You will be responsible for developing and executing the sourcing strategy for the Professional Services and HR categories, leading complex negotiations, and driving cost-saving initiatives while maintaining service quality.

We are looking for a proactive, organized, and collaborative individual who works well in a fast-paced environment. This role will report to the Manager, Strategic Sourcing -  Indirect. The is a hybrid role (2 days a week in office) and must be based in either  Bellevue, WA, or the San Francisco Bay Area.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Category Strategy & Planning: Develop and execute proactive sourcing strategies for the Professional Services, Facilities and/or HR categories, working closely with functional groups to understand their long-term needs and deliver tangible value.
  • Sourcing & Project Management: Manage the entire lifecycle of sourcing projects from end-to-end, including conducting RFIs, RFPs, and market assessments to lead supplier selection activities.
  • Contract Negotiation: Lead commercial term negotiations for project-level agreements (Statements of Work, Order Forms) and partner with Legal on Master Service Agreements (MSAs) or various Exhibits, ensuring favorable terms and compliance.
  • Cost Optimization: Identify and implement cost-saving opportunities through strategic negotiations and process improvements. Develop and track KPIs to measure savings and value.
  • Stakeholder Partnership:  Build strong relationships with stakeholders to understand their objectives and align procurement initiatives with their business requirements.
  • Supplier Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with key suppliers, ensuring they meet performance expectations, quality standards, and contractual agreements.
  • Process, Governance & Compliance: Ensure adherence to Okta’s SS&P policies and procedures. Help develop and implement governance to drive performance, mitigate risk, and improve operational efficiency.
  • Data Analysis & Reporting: Conduct internal and external data analysis to provide clear, actionable insights. Regularly update leadership on strategy progress, savings, and market trends.

Qualifications:

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business, Finance, or a related field.
  • 3-5 years of Strategic Sourcing experience within indirect/services categories and overall procurement experience.
  • Proven ability to lead contract and commercial term negotiations.
  • Independently run and manage RFPs.
  • Experience managing multiple projects in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and project management skills.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
  • Familiarity with the procure-to-pay process and proficiency in tools like Google Workspace and MS Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint). 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Professional certifications such as CSCP, CPSM, or CPIM.
  • Strong negotiation skills and experience in leading negotiations across various services and indirect categories
  • Ability to be responsible for multiple projects with tight target dates
  • Familiarity with the procure-to-pay process and vendor management principles
  • Excellent project management and time management skills
  • Proficiency with analytics and reporting
  • Experience with ERP and financial systems. Familiarity with Coupa, Workday/ScoutRFP is a plus

The Opportunity:

Are you a data-driven storyteller with a passion for transforming raw information into actionable insights that drive tangible business outcomes? Do you thrive on collaborating directly with business stakeholders to understand their needs and then architecting elegant data solutions? If so, we have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and motivated Manager-level Business Analytics to join our growing team in Bangalore.   • Drive Data Literacy: Educate and empower business users to effectively utilize dashboards and data insights to drive business outcomes.
  • Stay Ahead of the Curve: Continuously explore new data visualization techniques, analytical methodologies, data technologies, and AI capabilities for interactive analytics to enhance our analytics capabilities.
  • Collaborate and Communicate: Effectively communicate complex analytical findings and recommendations to stakeholders. Lead cross-functional collaborations to achieve project goals.
  • Data Governance & Quality: Champion data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across Finance, driving robust governance and maintaining core foundations such as the reporting inventory, structure, access, and data dictionary.
  • Performance Monitoring & Iteration: Monitor the performance and user adoption of developed dashboards, gather feedback, and implement necessary revisions for continuous improvement.
  • Documentation & Training: Develop comprehensive documentation for created dashboards and datasets. Provide training and support to business users to ensure effective utilization of analytics tools.

jobs.title: Benefits Manager, EMEA
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced EMEA Benefits Manager to oversee and optimize our regional benefit programs. Based in Dublin and reporting to our US-based leadership, you will lead the implementation, day-to-day administration, and compliance of Okta's Total Rewards offerings across the EMEA region.

The ideal candidate is a data-driven problem solver with strong business acumen, capable of translating complex data into financial projections and actionable strategy. You must be an execution-focused leader who excels at managing multiple deadlines, building cross-functional relationships, and influencing stakeholders at all levels of a global, matrixed organization.

What you’ll be doing

  • Deep Regional Expertise: Serve as the subject matter expert for complex, region-specific programs, particularly focusing on pension governance, healthcare trusts, and employee councils across EMEA.
  • Vendor Procurement & Governance: Own the full vendor lifecycle for regional insurance carriers, brokers, and third-party administrators. Lead comprehensive onboarding, overseeing contract negotiations, security protocols, and data privacy reviews, while driving ongoing accountability through SLAs and Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs).
  • Strategic Administration & Integration: Manage the end-to-end administration of health, retirement, and wellbeing initiatives. Drive seamless data exchange between vendors, payroll, and internal HR platforms to minimize administrative friction and maximize data integrity.
  • Compliance, Risk & Benchmarking: Proactively monitor the EMEA regulatory landscape and conduct regular audits to ensure strict compliance with local laws. Conduct regional market research to ensure offerings remain highly competitive.
  • Cross-Functional Partnership: Partner closely with EMEA Payroll, People Operations (POPS), Finance, and Legal to ensure flawless program delivery, accurate taxation, and cohesive alignment across G&A functions.
  • Data & AI Integration: Utilize Workday and ServiceNow to track and report on key metrics. Leverage AI tools to analyze plan utilization, forecast costs, and personalize employee communication.
  • Program Evolution: Challenge the status quo to streamline administrative burdens. Document, implement, and automate processes in close partnership with the People Operations team.
  • Employee Experience & Escalations: Act as the highest level of regional escalation for complex benefits inquiries, ensuring a high-touch, empathetic, and seamless experience for Okta employees.
  • Communication & Education: Act as a subject matter expert for the People & Places community, managers, and employees, ensuring clear, effective delivery of benefit updates and educational resources.
  • Global Strategy Alignment: Maintain the team’s global project portfolio via SmartSheets and assist with overarching global rewards strategy initiatives as required, ensuring EMEA is represented in global design.

What you’ll bring to the role

  • Experience: Degree in a related discipline with 8+ years of experience in a regional Benefits role within a multinational environment.
  • EMEA Expertise: In-depth knowledge of complex benefits regulations, labor laws, and tax compliance across the EMEA region.
  • Analytical & Financial Rigor: A metrics-focused mindset with the ability to interpret quantitative/qualitative data, project financial impacts, and translate insights into actionable business recommendations.
  • Execution & Project Management: Highly execution-driven with the ability to manage multiple, deadline-focused initiatives with high precision and autonomy in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
  • Relationship Building & Influence: A track record of building trust across a matrixed organization, with the communication skills to present information effectively and influence diverse stakeholders at all levels.

jobs.title: Associate Technical Account Manager - Auth0 Enterprise
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

Get to know Okta
Okta is The World’s Identity Company. We free everyone to safely use any technology—anywhere, on any device or app. Our Auth0 and Okta Platforms enable secure yet flexible access, authentication, and automation that transforms how people move through the digital world, putting Identity at the heart of business security and growth.

At Okta, we celebrate a variety of perspectives and experiences. We are not looking for someone who checks every single box, we’re looking for lifelong learners and people who can make us better with their unique experiences. 

Join our team! We’re building a world where Identity belongs to you. 

 

The Okta Technical Account Management Team
The TAM team empowers Okta customers to deliver secure, scalable, and transformative identity solutions by acting as their trusted identity coach. We drive lasting success through elevated technical maturity, proactively guiding their identity decisions and long-term identity vision. We guide a customer’s strategy for customer identity and security posture while driving value in Okta’s suite of Auth0 products and, ultimately, retention at contract renewal.

 

The Auth0 Technical Account Manager Opportunity
This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced technical expert to join the TAM team. This role specializes in the Auth0 platform, which is an easy-to-implement, adaptable authentication and authorization platform for Consumer and SaaS applications. We make your login experience awesome. You will be working with a portfolio of well-known brands on their Auth0 implementations as an identity coach. Building close relationships with technical stakeholders from early in the customer lifecycle, you will learn their business, goals, challenges and technical landscape, ensuring Okta delivers on a long-term strategic plan to help solve these challenges and realize the value of their Auth0 platform investment, while continuing to evolve to solve new objectives and improve security posture.

The ideal candidate for this role has experience in either customer business-facing or technical delivery. They exhibit both interest and adaptability to both technical and business discussions, including agility in conversation and intelligent follow-through for customers. They are able to showcase an understanding of the TAM role through concrete examples of problem solving strategies and tactics often faced in the role. 


What you’ll be doing:

  • Serve as a trusted Identity Coach, guiding customers to achieve their objectives through timely technical guidance, advice, enablement, and best practice sharing
  • Learn our customers’ business, gaining insights into their strategies, goals, and challenges - through the lens of identity
  • Build and nurture long-term relationships with stakeholder personas from developers, engineering leads, app builders, and product owners, through to C-level exec, evolving customers into Auth0 champions
  • Conduct quarterly customer meetings with executives to recap past value, show progress on current initiatives and future state or roadmap items
  • Conduct ad hoc customer meetings with technical practitioners on identity solutions, technical guidance and best practices
  • Monitor customer health, satisfaction, and engagement, proactively addressing risks and escalations
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to develop strategies to increase customer adoption, and uncover growth opportunities that will solidify Auth0 for the entirety of customer identity
  • Analyze trends, identify areas for improvement, and take action to optimize customer outcomes
  • Act as a product evangelist, educating customers on new features and alignment of roadmaps to enhance their experience
  • Represent the voice of the customer, ensuring their needs and feedback shape the company’s approach


Requirements:

  • 2+ years of total experience in information technology, with a Bachelor’s degree; or an advanced degree without experience; or equivalent experience.
  • Familiarity in the following core CIAM areas or technical competencies: 
    • Technologies and protocol

Build knowledge through achieving minimum certification levels and spending time staying up to date on solutions and roadmap.

jobs.title: AI Operations Lead
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.

The Role

Accelerate your career by joining Okta as our AI Operations Lead. In this mission-critical individual contributor role, you will serve as the central source of truth for all AI flows—both internal and product-facing—across the company. You will be the map-maker for our AI ecosystem, ensuring that as we innovate at the speed of SaaS, we maintain a clear, documented, and compliant understanding of what data fuels our AI and how it is used.

This is a high-autonomy role designed for a proactive operator who can navigate complex issues and build cross-functional bridges in a remote-first environment. You will report into the Sr. Director, AI Legal and work closely with Legal, Engineering, Privacy, and Security stakeholders.

What you’ll be doing

  • The Enterprise AI Command Center: Establish and oversee the enterprise-wide AI Asset Library, serving as the definitive, central source of truth for all models, internal workflows, and third-party integrations.
  • Revenue Acceleration & Path Clearing: Act as a strategic liaison between Legal, Engineering, and Sales to build cross-functional bridges that turn complex hurdles into competitive advantages, directly accelerating our market growth.
  • Data Provenance & Lineage Architecture: Map and document the entire lifecycle of data, from source origins to final usage, ensuring every AI output is explainable, audit-ready, and legally sound.
  • Operationalizing Ethics at Scale: Design and execute high-velocity operational workflows (AIIAs and Model Cards) to assess privacy, ethical, and legal risks, ensuring we reach production with speed and safety.
  • Strategic Vendor Gatekeeper: Maintain and evolve procurement processes to ensure every new AI tool meets our internal trust standards before it is integrated into our ecosystem.
  • Rigorous Third-Party Auditing: Conduct deep-dive operational audits of vendor AI capabilities, evaluating data retention and opt-out mechanisms to protect our most valuable company and customer data.
  • Policy-to-Playbook Transformation: Translate high-level governance into actionable, high-performance playbooks, ensuring compliance is built into our daily operations rather than treated as an afterthought.
  • Global Content Curation: Independently lead the effort to ensure our AI messaging remains consistent, clear, and authoritative both internally and for our global customer base.
  • Remote-First Internal Diplomacy: Expertly navigate complex stakeholder interests across our global organization to ensure AI initiatives move forward at pace without compromising security.
  • Critical Incident Response Leadership: Provide the essential data maps and asset intelligence needed by Legal and Security to resolve potential incidents with precision and speed.

What you’ll bring to the role

  • AI Fluency & Technical Intuition: You are an AI-first thinker. You have a practical understanding of how LLMs and Generative AI tools function. You aren’t intimidated by the black box of AI; instead, you are driven to document its boundaries and ensure its outputs are reliable and compliant.
  • 5+ years of experience in Operations, Privacy Ops, or Program Management within a fast-paced SaaS technology company.
  • Expert Knowledge of SaaS Ecosystems: You understand cloud architecture, APIs, and the nuances of how data moves in a multi-tenant environment.
  • Privacy-First Mindset: Significant experience with privacy frameworks (GDPR, CCPA) and a deep understanding of why data residency and usage rights are critical in the AI era.
  • Masterful Communication: Ability to navigate internal politics with grace, building consensus across diverse teams (from DevOps to Legal) without formal authority.
  • High Autonomy: Proven track record of thriving in a remote-first environment with little oversight; you are a self-starter who creates their own runbook.
  • Agility in Ambiguity:

Get to know Okta


Okta is The World’s Identity Company. We free everyone to safely use any technology—anywhere, on any device or app. Our Workforce and Customer Identity Clouds enable secure yet flexible access, authentication, and automation that transforms how people move through the digital world, putting Identity at the heart of business security and growth.

At Okta, we celebrate a variety of perspectives and experiences. We are not looking for someone who checks every single box - we’re looking for lifelong learners and people who can make us better with their unique experiences.

Join our team! We’re building a world where Identity belongs to you.

The SLED Sales Team

We are seeking a passionate, results oriented, sales professional to drive revenue growth calling on SLED accounts.  Our Account Executives are individual contributors who play a vital role in driving a significant share of revenue for Okta. We provide our reps with an environment in which they can make valuable contributions from day one while also building opportunities for learning and growth. 

The Account Executive, SLED Opportunity

Reporting to the Area Sales Director, this Sr. level role will drive the sales process for midsize enterprise customers (50-2000 employees). The right candidate for the position will enjoy closing new logos while simultaneously managing and expanding a book of install base customers. Our SLED AE’s organize and conduct sales presentations, site visits and product demonstrations to prospects and represent Okta in a consistent, effective and professional manner to best develop and win new clients.

What you’ll be doing 

  • Manage the sales process for medium-sized new logo customers from demo to contract negotiation
  • Expand business within existing Okta customers by building long-term strategic relationships with key accounts
  • Develop and execute against an assigned quota and territory plan
  • Prospect, forecast, build and maintain a sales pipeline with assigned Sales Development Representatives
  • Present to C-level executives in the field and via web demonstrations
  • Partner with ISV's and strategic partners to win revenue for Okta

What you’ll bring to the role

  • 5 years of SLED sales and account management experience in a SaaS/Cloud B2B environment
  • A proven track record of success selling in territory to mid-sized and/or enterprise customers
  • ISV or Channel experience strongly preferred  
  • IT/Security sales experience strongly preferred
  • This role must sit near our Chicago,IL office hub or in the region (IL, IA, MO, AR, OK).

  • BS/BA degree preferred

And extra credit if you have experience in any of the following! 

  • Cloud First 
  • Security

jobs.title: Customer Success Associate - LATAM
jobs.description:
As a Customer Success Associate, you will focus on the long term strategic relationship with our customers, drive adoption, and identify growth opportunities across a high volume of Datadog customers to ensure successful product use and retention. You’ll be an advocate for the customer internally and focus on providing a positive experience for a high volume of accounts. Associates follow a well-defined methodology to identify the customer’s unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. As we scale our Customer Success team, furthering personal development and team success is a top priority.

At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them

What You’ll Do:

  • Identify target opportunities, manage the full sales cycle and proactively build strategic relationships with customers and focus on retention.  
  • Manage requests from your book of business such as but not limited to technical support escalations, billing questions, documentation/enablement requests to ensure a positive customer experience
  • Pivot account management strategy based on different customer requirements and needs seamlessly and efficiently
  • Act as an advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the solution effectively
  • Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and support greater adoption rates
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with internal Datadog teams (support, product, finance, and legal)

Who You Are:

  • Customer-minded, always trying to provide the most value possible
  • Driven and motivated by a career in sales with a strong desire to grow a career in Customer Success.
  • A strong communicator (written and verbal) with excellent attention to detail
  • Experienced in independently negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections for up-sell/cross sell activities
  • 1+ years of experience in a customer account facing role and desire to grow a career in Customer Success
  • Passionate about building long term and lasting relationships

Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.

Benefits and Growth:

  • Best-in-breed onboarding
  • Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message 
  • An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds
  • Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking 
  • Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing 
  • New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)
  • Generous global benefits

Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.

Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.

The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:
$54,000—$72,000 USD

About Datadog: 

Datadog is the leading observability and security platform for the AI era, providing businesses with unified visibility across the technology stack to manage complexity at scale.
